title: calliopsis rosea
intro: Calliopsis rosea, also known as the goldeneye or false sunflower, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Calliopsis rosea include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and valuable resource in natural medicine.
